text: VO2max is fundamentally bounded by the Fick equation: stroke volume (end-diastolic volume minus end-systolic volume) multiplied by heart rate multiplied by arterio-venous oxygen difference.
VO2max is critically dependent on convective oxygen transport to working skeletal muscle, confirming that central cardiovascular limits are the primary constraint.
Elite endurance athletes achieve high VO2max primarily through high cardiac output enabled by a large, compliant cardiac chamber that relaxes quickly and fills to a large end-diastolic volume.

The large left ventricular filling and ejection capacity in elite athletes allows preservation of blood pressure during extraordinary rates of muscle blood flow, supporting high sustained oxidative metabolism.
Cardiac phenotype plasticity underlying VO2max adaptations is influenced by genetic factors, training duration, training type, training intensity, and the age at which training is initiated.

Peripheral muscle fatigue during maximal exercise results from energetic supply/demand mismatch at the skeletal muscle level, with afferent signalling pathways communicating these conditions to the brain for central integration of cardiovascular and neuromotor control.
